summary: Body copy font size minimum 16px; larger sizes preferred for accessibility.
Line height ≥150%; increase for light-on-dark text for accessibility.
Use a type scale to establish clear visual hierarchy for accessibility.
Ensure text legibility by testing contrast, never use color alone for information, and proof all text for accessibility compliance.
Forms require always visible labels; no placeholder-as-label or disappearing labels allowed.
Click/tap targets must be minimum 48px for accessibility compliance.
Use quick, non-disruptive animations with pause option; avoid excessive motion; ensure compatibility across phone, tablet, and desktop devices.
[GENERAL email] Animated GIFs must show key message in frame 1; no autoplay motion >5s without static fallback, ensuring Outlook compatibility.
Accessibility: Descriptive alt text required on all images; transcripts must be provided for all video and audio content.
Accessibility standards must comply with WCAG guidelines as referenced on wcag.com.
Confirm audience in every brief before writing patient (DTC) materials; language rules differ by audience.
Use 'someone/people with dermatomyositis' in patient materials; use 'patients' only in HCP materials.
The patient brochure is the master reference for visuals, messaging, and tone across all LISRAYA materials.
Each section is a 600px wide <table> block with 32px top/bottom padding, own background color, no outside margins; sections stack directly.
Section metadata must declare background color, DM establishment, footnote symbols used (†, ‡ assigned at assembly), and presence of CTA.
Footnote symbols are superscript, assigned sequentially at assembly; sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) for multiple referenced claims, not hard-coded symbols.
Locked header, footer, ISI inserted by core; adjacent sections must avoid decorative elements visually dependent on them (e.g., waves continuing into footer).
Dark-background sections cannot be adjacent; assembler ensures spacing via declared backgrounds.
Use HEX colors, px sizing, inline CSS only, duplicate bgcolor attributes for Outlook in assembly code.

LISRAYA colors: Primary (Brand Blue #00529b, Sunshine #faa31b, Gold #ffc60a) dominant over Secondary (Sky Blue #358CCB, Deep Blue #003D74, Dark Navy #011E45) over Tertiary (Coral #F26A38, Light Blue #E6F0F9, Golden Sand #FEF1C8, Graphite #212121, White #ffffff).
Apply palette with primary > secondary > tertiary ratio per guide; blues and golds dominate, tertiary colors used as accents and surfaces.
Headlines and headings use Brand Blue (#00529b) or White (#ffffff) on dark/gradient backgrounds.
Body text uses Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds, White on dark backgrounds; increased line height per §1.2 applies.
In charts, use Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) as dominant colors; gray for placebo; Sunshine/Gold restricted per chart_sunshine_gold_key_figures.
In charts, use Sunshine and Gold colors exclusively to emphasize key figures only.
Callout surfaces use Light Blue 70% tint for data callouts, Golden Sand 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.
Use coral only as a sparing tertiary accent; never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.
Avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief specifically calls for it.
Links are Brand Blue #00529b and underlined on light backgrounds; White and underlined on dark backgrounds.
Logo icon uses 70% transparent Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a in all instances, including logos and patterns/graphics.
Campaign headline letters "D" and "M" use ~40% tint of headline color (Gold or Brand Blue).
Light Blue callout boxes use 70% tint of Light Blue (#E6F0F9).
Golden Sand callout boxes use 30% tint of #FEF1C8 color.
Wave top layers require 70% transparency applied.
color_application: Use only palette values or specified tints—campaign headline letters ≈40%, Light Blue callout 70%, Golden Sand callout 30%, wave top layers 70% transparency.

Use only approved gradients: Brand Blue→Dark Navy (#00529b→#011E45), Sky Blue→Deep Blue (#358CCB→#003D74), Sunshine→75% Gold (#faa31b→Gold @75%), Golden Sand→White→Light Blue (#FEF1C8→#ffffff→#E6F0F9), Golden Sand→75% Gold→Sunshine (#FEF1C8→Gold @75%→#faa31b), Masthead Sunshine→Light Gold (#faa31b→≈#ffe05c).
Use Brand Blue Dark Navy gradient (#00529b→#011E45) on dark backgrounds and blue masthead/waves.
Use Sky Blue to Deep Blue gradient (#358CCB → #003D74) for blue backgrounds and waves.
Use The Sunshine 75% Gold gradient (#faa31b to Gold@75%) for warm backgrounds.
Use Golden Sand to White to Light Blue gradient (#FEF1C8→#ffffff→#E6F0F9) for soft light backgrounds.
Use Golden Sand → 75% Gold → Sunshine gradient (#FEF1C8 → Gold 75% → #faa31b) for warm light-to-saturated backgrounds.
Use Masthead gradient Sunshine to light gold (#faa31b to #ffe05c, 0C10M78Y0K) behind LISRAYA logo; logo placed at lightest gradient end.
In masthead/banner, apply horizontal gradient from dark/saturated (left) to light (right).
Ensure text/background contrast meets WCAG AA: ≥4.5:1 for body text, ≥3:1 for large text (≥24px or 19px bold), using webaim.com or similar.
Known-safe text/background pairs: Blue shades (#00529b, #003D74, #011E45, #212121) on White (#ffffff), Light Blue (#E6F0F9), Golden Sand (#FEF1C8); White text on these blues.
Avoid Gold (#ffc60a) or Sunshine (#faa31b) text on White (#ffffff) due to low contrast; use Golds only for surfaces, rules, or large display emphasis.
Avoid using color alone to convey meaning; always pair with icons, depth, clear text, or visual feedback for accessibility.
Gradient Callout CTA Section: full-width banner with branded orange/yellow gradient background, headline above and single button below, both horizontally centered and stacked.
Apply ast_lisraya_gradient_callout_bg PNG as section background, size cover, no-repeat, centered, full content width 600px.
Set solid background-color Sunshine #faa31b fallback on Gradient Callout section for Outlook clients dropping background images.
Gradient Callout background image from Solstice S3 must not be re-hosted or recolored.
Headline: Nunito Sans 800, 28px, centered top third, Brand Blue #00529b, single line.
cta button centered below headline, 248x64px, Deep Blue #003D74 fill, white Nunito Sans 800 18px label, Gold #FFC20E 24px arrow after two nbsp.
cta button uses border-radius: 49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px exactly on all instances, never simplified, corners swapped, or decimals rounded.
Skip background image only in rare cases; remove background-url, size, repeat, position, keep background-color #faa31b and maintain headline, button color, and radius exactly; all else unchanged.
Icon Callout Box: Light Blue #E6F0F9 fill, short blue headline, white #ffffff icon badge straddling left edge center, for 'proof point' statements.
Callout on white (#ffffff) background, blue box inset left by half badge width (W/2) so outer badge half fits; other content full width.
Use Light Blue #E6F0F9 fill for the box accent shape, vertically sized to text with generous padding.
Left padding equals badge width/2 plus 24px gap (e.g., 64px if W=80px) ensuring copy doesn't underlap the overhanging badge.
Callout text: Brand Blue #00529b, Nunito Sans 800 weight, ~21px size, 28px line-height, left-aligned, vertically centered in box.
Icon badge: White #ffffff rounded square with Brand Blue #00529b line icon, vertically centered, horizontally centered on left box edge, edge as badge's perpendicular bisector.

Badge width W=80px, inset blue box left by W/2=40px, badge center on box left edge with left:0 in padding-left:40px wrapper; vertical center top:50% margin-top:-H/2; if size differs, inset=W/2, box padding-left=W/2+24px, margin-top=-H/2.
Iconography box border-radius must be 40px top-left & bottom-right, 8px top-right & bottom-left (5:1 ratio); never altered, simplified, swapped, or flipped.
Iconography white badge mimics box accent shape with border-radius 20px 4px 20px 4px, maintaining 5:1 ratio large top-left and bottom-right; no uniform radius, swaps, or flips.
White icon badges require box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18) drop shadow to lift off blue box, always applied to avoid flat appearance.
Use pre-composited transparent PNG for 50/50 absolute-positioned badge to avoid Outlook desktop/Word engine issues; else accept badge flush inside left edge with other styles unchanged.
Email body width fixed at 600px centered; sections must render edge-to-edge exactly 600px for seamless assembly.
At 480px mobile breakpoint, all multi-column layouts collapse to single column.
Layout internal section grid: 12-column model; layouts include 1-col, 2-col (50/50), or image/text split (~40/60).
Section side padding is 24px left/right for all sections; full-bleed imagery/gradients may extend to 600px but text maintains 24px insets.
Use 8px vertical rhythm base; padding/margins multiples of 8; Heading→Body 16px; Paragraph→Paragraph 16px; Body→Callout/CTA 24px; Image→Caption 8px.
Section top/bottom internal padding = 32px each; sections have own padding; no external margins; stack with zero gaps.
Primary CTA: Brand Blue #00529b background, White #ffffff Nunito Sans Bold 16px text, padding 14x32px, corner radius 24px pill shape.
Buttons/CTAs require a minimum touch target height of 48px to meet brand accessibility standards.
Secondary CTA: white bg, 2px #00529b border, #00529b text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, padding 14x32px, radius 24px, ≥48px touch target.
One primary CTA allowed per section maximum.
Imagery wave forms allowed in Sunshine/Gold or Sky Blue/Deep Blue, each with long and short variants only.
Imagery waves: top wave layer at 70% opacity over base (Gold over Sunshine; Sky Blue over Deep Blue), preserving layered translucent effect.
Waves can be used alone or over photography/gradient backgrounds for flow, framing, or depth, only with approved color values.
Masthead uses Sunshine gradient #faa31b→#ffe05c (0C,10M,78Y,0K), logo mark placed at lightest end, for holding copy.
Imagery: Use warm, realistic patient lifestyle photos of everyday activities (walking dog, beach, BBQ) showing returning to normal life; use ast_lisraya_patient_lifestyle_photo asset.
Images must use Accent shape (§2.5), asymmetric rounded-corner crops, never plain rectangles or circles.
Waves and gradients can overlay photography.
All images must have descriptive alt text for accessibility compliance.
Brand Accent Shape: rectangle with diagonal corner radii pairs—large radius (1.25", 0.5", 0.4375" or 120px, 48px, 42px) plus 0.0625" (6px) on opposite corners; houses callout text/photos.
Maintain consistent diagonal accent shape orientation per email; default corners: large radius top-left & bottom-right, 6px radius on others.
Charts and tables use 12px uniform rounded corners on all sides, overriding the asymmetric accent shape exception.
Chart and infographic containers have 12px rounded corners, Light Blue (#E6F0F9) panel, and Brand Blue (#00529b) rounded header bar.
Use Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) as dominant colors for data in charts.
Use Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) only for emphasis on key figures/data points in charts.
Use neutral gray color for Placebo/comparator data in charts.
Chart bars and icons feature rounded tops for a friendly, approachable design.
Chart headlines use Agenda (print)/Nunito Sans Bold+ (email), ALL CAPS, Brand Blue (#00529b), with thin Gold rule beneath.
Statistical callouts feature large, bold % figures as heroes with lighter-weight supporting copy below.
Chart footnotes use smaller typography and distinct spacing to separate from primary data.
Icons must be minimal line-illustrations with consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color lines, and new icons must match this style.
Icon colors and backgrounds must be adjusted only within the brand palette.
Iconography must have minimum 32×32px size with appropriate scale and contrast for low-vision audiences in email.
Icon strokes default to Brand Blue (#00529b) on light backgrounds.
Ensure clean layouts with generous white space; limit focal points per section; use contrast to highlight the primary element.
Use imagery and icons to break up text walls, following the "Show, don't tell" layout rule.
Use alternating White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, or gradient backgrounds for color blocks in My Compass webpage sections to prevent visual merging of adjacent topics.
Section designers declare background color in metadata to prevent identical adjacent backgrounds during assembly sequencing.
Use bold headlines, simple benefit statements, and a patient quote as the canonical content block, following the patient brochure template for all LISRAYA materials.
Use LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all materials; this overrides Feb 2026 guide's title-case rule.
Include ® only with generic name on first/prominent mention in external documents; omit on subsequent mentions.
Generic name format: lowercase, enclosed in parentheses, e.g., (brepocitinib).
Parent company Priovant Therapeutics, Inc. sign-off only in locked footer; do not include in any sections.
In print materials, set headlines and headers in Agenda (Adobe font).
Use Nunito Sans font with bolder weights and Title Case for all digital/email headlines and headers.
Use Agenda font as live text exclusively for digital campaign headlines.
Use Nunito Sans for body copy, footers, and fine print in all print, digital, and email materials.
Use Aptos as fallback font in Word, PPT, Excel for headlines, headers, body copy, footers, and fine print.
Email CSS font stack: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; load Nunito Sans via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported for fallback compatibility.
Email typography: H1 Hero 28px/36px Nunito Sans ExtraBold/Bold; H2 22px/30px Bold; H3 18px/26px Bold/SemiBold; Body 16px/24px Regular; Secondary 14px/21px Regular; Footnote 12px/18px.
Body copy minimum font size: 16px (brand-mandated), preference for larger sizes.
Secondary/supporting body text uses 14px/21px line-height, used sparingly, never for primary content.
Footnote/reference text in a section must be 12px/18px, noticeably smaller and clearly separated from primary data.
Never set running text below 12px; ISI sizing controlled by locked component.
Line height minimum 150% for all body copy to ensure brand accessibility compliance.
Increase line height to 160% (e.g., 16px/26px) for light text on dark backgrounds.
Use clear type scale and visual hierarchy to guide reader's attention in order: first, next, last.
Avoid adjacent text blocks with identical size and weight to differentiate hierarchy levels.
Headlines/headings use title case: capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions (e.g., "LISRAYA Targets the Root Cause").
Campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' must always be in ALL CAPS as per rule 1.6.
Chart/infographic headlines use ALL CAPS; Agenda font for print, Nunito Sans Bold or heavier for email fallback.
Body copy text uses sentence case formatting.
No ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheads except 'YOU DESERVE MORE' campaign and chart/infographic headlines only.
Use bold only for hierarchy/key takeaways: per email section, bold headings, one key phrase/claim per paragraph, and statistical callout figures only.

applies_when.value: brand name within a benefit claim, first instance per section

summary: Always bold callout text (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, and first-instance brand name in benefit claims per section.
Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, over ~15% running body text per section, prepositional filler, or generic names.
Avoid using bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis together on the same string except in chart headlines and campaign headline lockups; limit to two emphasis types per element.
No underlining text except on genuine hyperlinks.
No italics for emphasis in patient copy; use italics only for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
Use "dermatomyositis (DM)" at first mention, then "DM" in running copy to avoid repetition.
Use full word "dermatomyositis" in all headlines, headings, subheads; never use "DM" for clarity, accessibility, and SEO.
Each standalone section must define 'dermatomyositis (DM)' at first mention unless assembly brief confirms abbreviation established upstream.
Use "someone/people with dermatomyositis" in patient materials; use "patients" only in HCP materials.
Use "care team" instead of "staff" to refer to patients, HCPs, and support systems.
Spell out numbers zero–nine; use numerals 10+; always use numerals for percentages (% sign), ages, and ratios.
Use empathetic, supportive tone avoiding negative/fear language in clinical data citations; maintain trust and detail without oversimplifying.
Use Oxford/serial comma in series of 3 or more items.
Campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' uses Agenda; digital allows live Agenda, email fallback to Nunito Sans ExtraBold only.
Campaign headline "YOU DESERVE MORE" must always be in ALL CAPS.
In campaign headline, letters 'D' (DESERVE) and 'M' (MORE) use ~40% tint of headline color, referencing 'd' and 'm' in dermatomyositis.
Campaign headline colors: brand Gold/Sunshine or Brand Blue, tinted letters at 40% hue only.
Hero placements stack campaign headline in three left-aligned lines: YOU / DESERVE / MORE.
copy_editorial: Campaign lockup copy must exactly read "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
Use three approved callout styles judiciously; never place two callouts of the same style competing on one screen or section.
Main callout uses centered bold Brand Blue (#00529b) text with Gold (#ffc60a) horizontal rules above and below.
[GENERAL] Main callout horizontal rules: 2px weight, width 60–100% of text block, 16px padding between rule and text.
Secondary/data callout: Light Blue (#E6F0F9) box 70% tint with Bold Brand Blue text, optional one-line icon (e.g., 1x pill icon).
Secondary/data callout may include up to one line icon (e.g., 1x pill icon) only.
[GENERAL] Secondary/data callout box: 20px internal padding, corner radius per accent-shape rules (§2.5).
Text-heavy callout uses Golden Sand #FEF1C8 30% tint box, Bold Brand Blue #00529b headline, line icons, Bold Sky Blue #358CCB subheads, Regular Graphite #212121 body text.
Text-heavy callout: 24px internal padding, two-column icon+text layout, collapses to single column on mobile.
copy_editorial: Use approved campaign headline verbatim: "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

Use product benefits framework verbatim: "LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks inflammatory signals driving dermatomyositis, so you can:" + bullet points: Increase muscle strength, Improve skin symptoms, Rely less on steroids, Do more activities you love.
Primary MOA statement must be used verbatim: "Unlike other treatments for dermatomyositis (DM), which suppress the immune system generally, LISRAYA targets the root cause of the disease.
Alternate MOA statement must be used verbatim: "...LISRAYA works by turning off the specific inflammatory switches that are stuck in the 'on' position.
Use the exact administration statement: "LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.
Doctor-discussion CTA must exactly read: "Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.
Patient support program name must be exactly "My Compass Support" covering Personalized Communication via PAL: text, phone, email, in person; Authorization Support.
Voice tone rules: confident, clear first-of-its-kind claims, never boastful, overstated, or sensationalized.
Voice is warm, genuine empathy-focused, avoiding over-emotion; emphasizes DM's daily impact and centers on patients.
Use clear, plain, respectful language that is easy to understand without losing accuracy.
Tone flexes slightly by context; core voice remains constant.
Clinical claims need † footnote markers; footnotes separated and smaller than body copy as per §1.2.
Patient quotes attributed as - [Name], LISRAYA patient, italicized or visually distinct, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.
Patient quotes must be paired with warm lifestyle imagery.
Priovant logo, copyright, ® line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, ISI live only in locked header/footer; never reproduced in modular sections.
Use brand colors in order: #00529B Deep Blue (dominant), #FAA31B Amber Orange (secondary accent), #FFC60A Golden Yellow (tertiary accent).
Use Agenda font for all headlines and headers as the primary headline typography.
Use Nunito Sans for all print body copy.
CTA buttons: use 180x59 px SVG with asymmetric rounded path filled by horizontal linear gradient #FFDF55 to #FAA21B.
